news.source: ABC News & Headlines – Australian Broadcasting Corporation

news.title: Wallabies vs Ireland live: Captain’s try scrubbed out in contentious obstruction call

sections.heading: Virimi Vakatawa handed Fiji debut
sections.body: Former France international Virimi Vakatawa makes his Fiji debut in today's midfield, returning to the Test arena. The center, who earned 32 caps for France, joins a reshaped backline alongside fly-half Caleb Muntz and scrum-half Frank Lomani. His presence adds world-class experience as the Flying Fijians look to bounce back from last weekend's heavy defeat.

sections.heading: Squad prioritizes physical conditioning
sections.body: Fiji's training camp has prioritized physical recovery and conditioning after a grueling start to the tournament. Without any medical updates in today's coverage, the squad's focus has shifted entirely to defensive organization and training intensity. Coaches have demanded a high-tempo response in sessions to address the defensive lapses from last week, ensuring the players are fully prepared for a physical battle.

sections.heading: Scotland make 14 changes
sections.body: Townsend has made 14 changes to his starting line-up, selecting a fresh Scotland side that features first-time starts for hooker Gregor Hiddleston and flanker Freddy Douglas. The head coach expects a massive contest at the breakdown, highlighting Douglas's jackaling skills to counter Fiji's threat. Dangerous wings Darcy Graham and Duhan van der Merwe return, indicating Scotland's intent to play a highly expansive game.

sections.heading: Rebuilding pride at Murrayfield
sections.body: Fiji enter today's fixture seeking their first competition points after two tough opening defeats. Rebounding from the heavy loss to England is crucial to restoring defensive pride before the tournament pauses. A positive result against the Scots would provide a massive boost, helping the Flying Fijians climb the standings as they prepare for the autumn stage of the championship.

sections.heading: Borthwick names side for Santiago del Estero
sections.body: Steve Borthwick has named his matchday squad for the fixture in Santiago del Estero. Joe Heyes and Alex Coles are included in the starting pack, while Fin Smith continues at fly-half. Despite scoring three tries as a substitute against Fiji, Henry Pollock is named on the bench alongside Tom Curry. The backline features Seb Atkinson and Henry Slade in the midfield.

sections.heading: Squad forced to move hotels in Buenos Aires
sections.body: The squad was forced to switch hotels in Buenos Aires to avoid mass celebrations and fireworks following the football World Cup semi-final between England and Argentina. While millions of fans took to the streets until the early hours, today's coverage does not specify how the move or the noise affected the physical condition or the training schedule of the rugby players.

sections.heading: Two changes for Pumas as rugby interest grows
sections.body: Argentina head coach Felipe Contepomi has made two changes to the starting lineup that defeated Wales in the previous round. The Pumas side includes captain Julian Montoya at hooker and Santiago Carreras at full-back. Following recent victories over South Africa and New Zealand, the hosts enter this match with Contepomi stating that rugby union is currently booming across the country.

sections.heading: England seeks upward movement in standings
sections.body: England enters the match in seventh place following their victory over Fiji. After losing their opening match against South Africa, Borthwick's side needs a result to move up the table. Northern Hemisphere rivals Ireland and France currently occupy the top positions. The final score will determine where England sits heading into the final stages of the summer window.

match.venue: Estadio Único Madre de Ciudades, Santiago del Estero

sections.heading: Jalibert starts at fullback
sections.body: Matthieu Jalibert returns to the starting XV at fullback. After missing training sessions earlier in the week, his inclusion provides stability for a side ranked No. 4 in the world. The selection features an experienced spine, with Romain Ntamack and Maxime Lucu directing play from the halfback positions while Emmanuel Meafou anchors the pack for this third-round encounter in Tokyo.

sections.heading: Penaud remains unavailable
sections.body: Damian Penaud remains the most significant absentee after being ruled out of the matches against Australia and Japan with a calf injury sustained in the opening round. No fresh injury news appears in today’s coverage following the announcement that Matthieu Jalibert is fit to play. The squad enters the match carrying the momentum of a 42-26 victory over Australia in the previous round.

sections.heading: Jones overhauls front row
sections.body: Japan head coach Eddie Jones has overhauled his front row, making six total changes to the side that lost to Ireland last weekend. The Brave Blossoms, currently ranked No. 11, rely on veterans like Warner Dearns and Jack Cornelsen to provide resistance. Despite their recent improvement, they have lost four of their last five meetings against France and face a significant physical challenge today.

sections.heading: Pressure to keep pace
sections.body: France goes into the weekend in fourth position after a bounce-back victory in the previous round. With a record of one win and one loss, Les Bleus must secure a result in Tokyo to stay within touching distance of the tournament leaders. A win would maintain France's status as a title contender before the competition resumes in the Northern Hemisphere this autumn.

sections.heading: Farrell rotates nine for Auckland finale
sections.body: Andy Farrell has shuffled his deck for the season finale, making nine changes to the side that defeated Japan. Captain Dan Sheehan returns to lead a pack bolstered by Tadhg Furlong and Josh van der Flier. Sam Prendergast retains the No. 10 shirt, while assistant coach Andrew Goodman challenged the squad to 'put our game on New Zealand' at their Auckland stronghold.

sections.heading: Clean bill of health for traveling squad
sections.body: While missing key names like Caelan Doris and Andrew Porter throughout this tour, the traveling squad has maintained a clean bill of health this week. Andy Farrell confirmed he is pleased to have all 36 squad members training and available for selection. Captain Dan Sheehan noted that while performances have been patchy, this fixture represents a 'brilliant opportunity' to bring everything together for the finale.

sections.heading: Rennie hands Ruben Love starting debut
sections.body: Dave Rennie has handed rookie fly-half Ruben Love a starting debut as the All Blacks look to extend their 52-match unbeaten streak at Eden Park. New Zealand have made five changes, shifting Tupou Vaa'i to the back row to add physical presence. The hosts lead the tournament in defenders beaten with 43, posing a significant threat to an Irish defense currently ranked second for tackle success.

sections.heading: Perfect tour on the line at Eden Park
sections.body: Ireland goes into the weekend leading the Northern Hemisphere standings after consecutive wins over Australia and Japan. A victory in Auckland would secure a perfect southern hemisphere tour and place the side in a commanding position for the inaugural Nations Championship Final. Having won five games in a row, the squad aims to end the season by making history at New Zealand’s cathedral of rugby.

sections.heading: Nine changes as Australian-born duo return home
sections.body: Italy have shuffled the deck with nine changes for the trip to Perth following consecutive defeats to Japan and New Zealand. The selection highlights the squad's Australian connections, with Melbourne-born Monty Ioane and Louis Lynagh—son of Wallabies legend Michael—both named in the starting backline. The pair combined for tries in November's win in Udine and remain central to the coaching staff's tactical plans.

sections.heading: Cannone begins four-week suspension
sections.body: Lorenzo Cannone is ruled out of Saturday’s Test after receiving a four-week ban for head-butting New Zealand’s Cam Roigard during last weekend's Round 2 defeat. With the remainder of the squad available for selection, training in Perth has focused on high-intensity defensive sets to counter the Wallabies' width. The Azzurri back row must now adjust to the absence of the No. 8.

sections.heading: Tizzano leads Schmidt’s final Wallabies bow
sections.body: Australia name a side determined to secure Joe Schmidt a winning farewell in his final Test before handing over to Les Kiss. Perth local Carlo Tizzano earns a start at openside flanker in place of Fraser McReight, joining a back row featuring Harry Wilson and Rob Valetini. Despite losing their opening fixtures to Ireland and France, the Wallabies represent a significant threat at HBF Park.

sections.heading: Quest for first win moves to Perth
sections.body: Italy entered the weekend sitting 11th in the table, one of several teams still searching for a maiden Nations Championship victory. A win today would secure a historic first ever Test triumph on Australian soil and provide vital momentum before the tournament resumes in November. With the Wallabies also winless after two rounds, this battle is critical for avoiding a last-place finish.

sections.heading: McDowall leads 14 changes for Murrayfield clash
sections.body: Stafford McDowall captains a vastly rotated Scotland side today, with Gregor Townsend making 14 changes to the starting XV. Only Gregor Brown retains his place from the defeat against South Africa. Townsend stated he is mindful of “creating depth” for the future, handing first international starts to hooker Gregor Hiddleston and flanker Freddy Douglas as Scotland look to close the series with a win.

sections.heading: Macbeth ruled out of final summer Test
sections.body: Nathan Macbeth is the only player ruled out of today's Test, having suffered a leg infection in recent days. Outside of his absence, there is no further injury news in today's coverage regarding the squad. The wider group has been training throughout the summer series, with the coaching staff focusing on integrating the full touring party into the match-day environment for today’s historic fixture.

sections.heading: Vakatawa set for debut as Fiji seek response
sections.body: Fiji have named an attacking side for today’s encounter, headlined by the international return of center Virimi Vakatawa. The Flying Fijians arrive in Edinburgh looking to recover from a heavy defeat to England last weekend. With the official lineup featuring Frank Lomani and Isaiah Armstrong-Ravula, Fiji will look to challenge Scotland at the breakdown—an area where Townsend expects Freddy Douglas to play a key role.

sections.heading: Record crowd gathers for final summer series fixture
sections.body: Today’s match marks a historic occasion, with ticket sales at Scottish Gas Murrayfield surpassing the previous record of 50,209. Scotland enters this final summer fixture aiming to bounce back from last week’s result against the Springboks. While the side sits in a competitive position in the Northern Hemisphere standings, the focus remains on securing a victory to conclude the series on a high note.

sections.heading: Tandy shakes up backline for Durban challenge
sections.body: Steve Tandy has made six changes for today’s encounter, introducing an entirely new midfield pairing. Ben Thomas joins Max Llewellyn in the centres, while Dan Edwards is drafted in at fly-half. In the back three, Louis Rees-Zammit and Blair Murray return to the starting side. Tandy has opted for a 6-2 split on the bench to manage the physical demands of the Springbok pack.

sections.heading: Lake cleared to lead in final tour fixture
sections.body: Captain Dewi Lake has been passed fit to start after leaving the field during last weekend’s defeat to Argentina. While Lake is available, the squad has been bolstered by the arrival of hooker Elliot Dee and forward Freddie Thomas, who were called up earlier this week as cover. The side is otherwise focusing on recovery following a challenging week of travel to the South African coast.

sections.heading: Springboks name four debutants for home test
sections.body: Rassie Erasmus has rung the changes, naming four uncapped players in his starting side: Carlu Sadie, Ruben van Heerden, Vusi Moyo, and Jaco Williams. The world champions, who have dominated recent meetings between the nations, head into the fixture unbeaten in the tournament thus far. They will rely on an experienced core including Malcolm Marx and Pieter-Steph du Toit to dictate the pace at Kings Park.

sections.heading: Final push to close out summer campaign
sections.body: Following a difficult loss in San Juan, Wales look to end the summer on a positive note against the world champions. With five points from their opening two matches, Steve Tandy’s side faces an uphill battle to climb the log. The squad is aiming for a cohesive performance to validate their progress after what has been a long and testing 10-month professional campaign.

sections.heading: Two alterations for Pumas side
sections.body: Argentina enters tonight’s contest with two adjustments to the starting lineup that secured victory over Wales last round. Head coach Felipe Contepomi, who recently noted that rugby is booming in the football-mad nation, looks to capitalize on momentum against a persistent rival. The Pumas are aiming to break a long-standing series trend, focusing on their traditional strength—a formidable scrum—to challenge the English pack tonight.

sections.heading: Squad conditioning remains consistent
sections.body: There is no fresh injury news in today’s coverage following the win against Wales. The squad has focused on tactical refinement and maintaining physical intensity throughout the week to ensure all players are prepared for the physical demands of this fixture. The camp is concentrated on the technical execution of their set-piece game, aiming to maintain the high standards shown in their recent performance.

sections.heading: England confirms unchanged starting XV
sections.body: England arrives with an unchanged lineup after their dominant 73-8 victory over Fiji. Steve Borthwick has retained his core, including Jamie George, who has publicly addressed the need for a protocol against potential racial abuse in the stands. Tactically, England seeks to continue their success from last week while remaining wary of the Pumas' historical prowess in the scrum, described as a primary target for pressure.

sections.heading: High tension in Santiago del Estero
sections.body: This fixture marks a pivotal moment for both teams in the middle of the log. Following their victory over Wales, Argentina seeks to climb the table by overcoming a difficult opponent. With the backdrop of a heated recent football rivalry between these nations, tonight’s match carries significant weight. A win would be a major statement for Los Pumas as they strive to move up the rankings.

match.venue: Estadio Único Madre de Ciudades, Santiago del Estero

sections.heading: Tizzano handed homecoming start
sections.body: Carlo Tizzano will start in his hometown of Perth, replacing Fraser McReight on the side of the scrum. The selection marks a significant change for Joe Schmidt in his final Test as head coach. Schmidt, who is set to hand over responsibilities to Les Kiss following this match, admits he is "nervous and a little bit lost" as he prepares to conclude his tenure.

sections.heading: Squad status for final summer fixture
sections.body: There is no fresh injury news in today's coverage regarding the Wallabies camp. The team has finalized their preparations for this evening’s encounter, entering the match with the squad listed below as they aim to improve upon consecutive losses to Ireland and France.

sections.heading: Italy ring changes for Perth visit
sections.body: Italy have made 9 changes to their side following defeats to Japan and New Zealand. Melbourne-born winger Monty Ioane returns to his home city to face the Wallabies, looking to replicate the success Italy found in their last two meetings with Australia. Although Italy has won those recent encounters, they have historically struggled to secure a victory on Australian soil.

sections.heading: Chasing a first tournament win
sections.body: Both Australia and Italy enter this clash searching for their first victory of the Nations Championship. For the Wallabies, the result carries additional weight as it serves as the final match of the summer window and the conclusion of the Joe Schmidt era. A win is vital to avoid a win-loss record falling below 36% for the departing coach.

sections.heading: Strategic reshuffle for French challenge
sections.body: Japan has made five changes to the starting XV for today’s fixture, including a complete overhaul of the front row. Captain Warner Dearns continues to lead the side, joined in the pack by Esei Haangana and Ben Gunter. The coaching staff has looked to inject fresh tactical energy into the lineup, with Takuro Matsunaga and Kazuma Ueda both earning starts to counter the visiting French side.

sections.heading: Conditioning and squad rotation focus
sections.body: There is no fresh injury news in today's coverage regarding the Japan squad. The focus in the build-up has remained on tactical adjustments and managing the conditioning of the group following the travel from last weekend’s fixture. The bench, bolstered by the inclusion of experienced campaigner Michael Leitch, provides significant cover and late-game impact options for the coaching staff as they seek to sustain intensity.

sections.heading: Les Bleus name heavy-hitting lineup
sections.body: France has confirmed a formidable starting XV featuring an experienced spine of Romain Ntamack, Maxime Lucu, Emmanuel Meafou, and Matthieu Jalibert. Despite recent injury concerns for Jalibert, he has been cleared to start at fullback. The visitors arrive in Tokyo looking to build on their victory over Australia, maintaining the momentum that currently places them among the tournament favorites in the standings.

sections.heading: Finding consistency in Tokyo
sections.body: After securing an opening win against Italy, Japan aims to bounce back from last weekend’s loss to Ireland. The Brave Blossoms currently sit at nine of 12 in the log, and a performance against the fourth-ranked French side is vital to climb the standings. With three rounds of the summer window now reaching their conclusion, every point earned today remains critical for the tournament's overall trajectory.

sections.heading: Rennie makes four changes for Eden Park showdown
sections.body: Coach Dave Rennie has reshuffled his pack for today’s fixture against Ireland. Tupou Vaa'i shifts to the back row, while Josh Lord and Patrick Tuipulotu are brought into the second row. In the backline, Quinn Tupaea earns a start in the midfield alongside Jordie Barrett. These four adjustments follow the team’s clinical 47-17 victory over Italy in the previous round.

sections.heading: Squad status remains unconfirmed
sections.body: There is no specific injury news regarding the New Zealand squad in today's coverage. The team heads into this final clash of the southern hemisphere leg of the Nations Championship with preparations focused on tactical adjustments in the pack and securing a third consecutive victory of the tournament.

sections.heading: Ireland name experienced side for final Test
sections.body: Ireland have recalled several heavy hitters for the Auckland clash, including Dan Sheehan, Tadhg Furlong, Josh van der Flier, and Jamison Gibson-Park. Sam Prendergast starts at fly-half, with Jimmy O'Brien selected on the wing. Captain Dan Sheehan noted that Ireland have a “brilliant opportunity” to finish their season on a high by producing their best performance of the year against the All Blacks.

sections.heading: Unbeaten records on the line in Auckland
sections.body: Both nations enter today’s contest with perfect 2-0 records in the competition. The All Blacks are looking to maintain a 52-match unbeaten streak at Eden Park, while Ireland aim to build on their opening wins against Australia and Japan. The outcome of this match will prove decisive for both sides as they push for a place in the inaugural Nations Championship Final.

sections.heading: Erasmus rotates squad for final hurdle
sections.body: Rassie Erasmus has made 10 changes to the side that defeated Scotland, opting to blood new talent in Durban. The matchday 23 features four uncapped players: Jaco Williams on the wing, fly-half Vusi Moyo, lock Ruben van Heerden, and prop Carlu Sadie. The selection retains an experienced core, including Malcolm Marx and Pieter-Steph du Toit, as the side looks to maintain its perfect tournament record.

sections.heading: Full squad available for selection
sections.body: There is no fresh injury news in today's coverage as the Springboks prepare for their final outing of the campaign. The squad has focused on tactical integration during training this week, ensuring the four debutants are aligned with the team's established structures. Following the victory over Scotland, the camp has remained focused on conditioning and maintaining the high intensity required for the weekend.

sections.heading: Tandy names side as Lake returns
sections.body: Steve Tandy has made six changes to the Wales starting XV, including an entirely new midfield pairing of Ben Thomas and Max Llewellyn. Crucially, captain Dewi Lake has been cleared to start after concerns over his fitness following the loss to Argentina. Wales have opted for a 6-2 bench split in an attempt to match the Springboks' physicality in the final quarter.

sections.heading: Top spot maintained heading into kickoff
sections.body: South Africa currently leads the Southern Hemisphere standings after impressive wins against England and Scotland. A victory today would further solidify their position at the summit of the table and the World Rugby rankings. With the tournament race intensifying, the Springboks will be looking to preserve their unbeaten status before the final rounds of the competition conclude.
